title: Japan's Noetra to work with Nvidia for physical AI infrastructure development | NHK WORLD-JAPAN News
title_zh: 日本Noetra将与英伟达合作开发物理AI基础设施
full_text:
Japan's Noetra to work with Nvidia for physical AI infrastructure development
A newly launched Japanese company says it will develop core technologies for physical AI by leveraging computing infrastructure equipped with US semiconductor giant Nvidia's latest chips.
Physical AI operates robots and other systems by perceiving surroundings and making autonomous decisions. The technology is expected to be deployed across a wide range of sectors, including manufacturers facing labor shortages.
Noetra was jointly established by Sony Group, SoftBank, NEC, Honda Motor and other major firms with the aim to develop homegrown AI.
Noetra is among the entities selected by Japan's industry ministry for a project to develop a domestic foundation model for physical AI.
The project is worth more than 380 billion yen, or over 2.34 billion dollars. It will be commissioned during the current fiscal year ending in March next year.
The ministry also plans to provide assistance of 1 trillion yen, or roughly 6.15 billion dollars, over five years through fiscal 2030. The sum includes funding to be allocated this fiscal year.
Noetra says it aims to accelerate development of the foundation model by using Nvidia's computing infrastructure. A key question is whether the project will enhance Japan's industrial competitiveness.
Noetra President and CEO Tamba Hironobu told NHK in an interview that the company can vie for the top spot in physical AI as the field is still at a competitive stage. He added that Japan can win the race because the country's strength in craftsmanship gives it the edge.
